summary: Package hijacks the `node` binary name and opens an attacker URL when build scripts invoke node
details: package.json declares `bin: { "node": "./shim.js" }`, which causes npm to place a `node` entry in `node_modules/.bin/` at install time. npm prepends `node_modules/.bin` to PATH for any `npm run` script, so any subsequent build step that invokes `node...` resolves to this shim instead of the real Node.js runtime. The shim (shim.js) imports child_process and platform-branches to `open` / `cmd /c start` / `xdg-open` against a hardcoded URL at https://github.com/X3r0Day/BunnyHijack, then calls `process.exit(0)` without executing the intended node code. The net effect on a consumer: every `npm run <script>` that shells out to `node` silently opens an attacker-controlled URL in the developer's browser and reports success while the real build step is skipped, producing both a browser-navigation side channel and silent sabotage of the build pipeline. The `BunnyHijack` naming and the deliberate shadowing of a core runtime binary make intent unambiguous.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
